free_text: Interaction of 28-GeV protons with uranium: charge and
mass distribution of rare-earth products
Each target consisted of a 0.002-in. thick uranium
foil and sandwiched between two stacks of three
0.001-in. aluminum foils.
Corrections for losses from the target foil by recoil
were estimated. The contributions to measured activi-
ties from low-energy secondaries produced in the
target had to Be taken into account.
The uncertainty (standard deviation) was
arrived at by root-mean-square combination of the
various individual sources of error, which were as
follows:
Counting statistics and decay curve
analysis (mostly < 5%, in a few instances up to 25%)
Counting efficiencies (5-10%);
Photopeak summing correction (uncertainty
estimated as 25% of the correction, I.e., 0-7%);
Chemical yield determinations (3-5%);
Secondary contribution to Na-24 in monitor (2%)
Secondary contribution to neutron-excess
yields (5%);
Recoil loss (1-2%);
Separation-time uncertainty (0-15%).
Data taken from PR/C,4,2202,1971, Tables I
and II.
BIB corrections.
OS. Corrections in DECAY-DATA and ISOMER in
subs.2,3, ERR-ANALYS updated, converted to lower case
BP: Added related reference and doi.
Comment by compiler: The 4.1 hr activity of Tb-149
was assigned by authors to Tb-149m
Isomer assignment of Tb-149 corrected,
DATA-ERR changed to ERR-T
Comment by compiler:
Authors give value 12.6 hr which corresponds to
Eu-150m. Modern value (36.9 yr) is given neither for
ground nor metastable state.
Comment by compiler:
Authors give value 5 yr.
Element value corrected.
Isomer assignments of Eu-150 and half-life
of Lu-172 corrected, DATA-ERR changed to ERR-T
